content.full_text:
January 29, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J St., Suite 600  
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Cassady,

This letter is meant to notify the commission of SANE/FREEZE’s intention to work on a California statewide ballot initiative known as the initiative to "Cut military spending and rebuild America."

SANE/FREEZE is a national political lobby with approximately 170,000 members. We are incorporated as a 501 C-4, nonprofit, tax-exempt political lobby. We are registered as such with both the Federal government and the state of California.

Our contribution to the aforementioned ballot initiative campaign will be in the form of gathering signatures towards its’ qualification for the November 1990 ballot. In addition, we will be asking people if they would like to work as volunteers on the initiative campaign. We will not be raising funds specifically for the initiative. We will consider our contribution to be a voluntary effort as we will not require our staff to carry initiative petitions and their normal job descriptions as canvassers for SANE/FREEZE will remain unchanged.

We understand, as covered in our conversation of 1/22/90, that within twenty calendar days of filing for qualification, the initiative committee must disclose the value of our contribution, based on the fair market value of signature gathering in California. We further understand that we will identify that market value by accounting for the actual amount of time spent by our staff both gathering petition signatures and travelling to the neighborhoods where we will be canvassing.

If we have misunderstood any aspect of our legal requirements in working to assist this ballot measure, please do not hesitate to call. We thank you in advance for your help and cooperation.

Yours Sincerely,

[Signature]
Peter Ferenbach
SANE/FREEZE
347 Dolores St. #335
San Francisco, CA 94110
(415)558-0695
